answer: Argon2id. Memory: 64MB, iterations: 3, parallelism: 4.
7 days, rotates on every use.
PostgreSQL 15.4, upgraded from PostgreSQL 14.9.
5000 req/s burst, 2000 req/s steady state.
Argon2id was chosen for resistance to side-channel attacks.
Drizzle is used for zero runtime dependencies and SQL-like TypeScript output.
They validate RS256 JWTs via the JWKS endpoint at `/.well-known/jwks.json` (also used by API Gateway Lambda authorizer).
Rate limiting is at ALB to provide DDoS protection before requests reach containers.
Request with JWT hits API Gateway; Lambda authorizer validates via JWKS and injects claims; Portfolio Service RBAC checks role from injected claims before rebalance.

If JWKS is down, Portfolio, Notification, and API Gateway Lambda cannot validate tokens; Auth can still issue tokens with its local private key; LRU cache (1000 entries, 5-minute TTL) provides a brief grace period.
Real-time alerting depends on both Notification Service WebSocket support and API Gateway WebSocket API; status is planned for Q3, while current threshold alerts are non-real-time.
Yes. The UTC+13/UTC+14 expiry bug was fixed by using Unix timestamps instead of Date-object timezone handling.
Use Notification webhooks for Auth MFA/login events: register enterprise webhook endpoints and configure Auth Service to emit those events into the Notification webhook channel.
100 maximum (20 connections per Auth instance × 5 instances).
Large tax-lot loads can exhaust Celery worker memory; the 20-connection pool limit constrains concurrent heavy recalculations; Spot interruption can terminate recalcs mid-run.
No. Spot is batch-only; Auth requires always-on availability and zero-downtime blue-green constraints, while Spot is interruptible.
Stateless auth doctrine: no server-side sessions/in-memory session state; JWT carries claims for horizontally scalable multi-service verification.
RPO is 1 hour and RTO is 4 hours; 30-day retention far exceeds RPO needs and supports longer recovery windows; cross-region replication to us-west-2 supports recovery objectives.
Add `?sslmode=verify-full&sslrootcert=global-bundle.pem` to the DB connection string.
`id, account_id, security_id, acquisition_date, cost_basis, quantity, adjusted, created_at`.
After credential validation in `handlePostLogin`, MFA verification is required when enabled (TOTP/recovery path), including setup via `POST /api/v1/auth/mfa/setup` for QR URI.
10 single-use recovery codes are generated at setup; each is permanently consumed when used.
Exponential backoff retry policy: 5 attempts maximum over 24 hours.
At rest: AES-256-GCM per-tenant encryption and Argon2id (64MB/3/4) for passwords. In transit: Envoy mTLS between services and PostgreSQL `sslmode=verify-full`. Secrets rotate every 90 days.

API Gateway schema validation -> Lambda authorizer (JWKS) -> VPC Link -> ALB (1000/min/IP) -> `handlePostLogin` -> Argon2id password verify -> MFA/TOTP check -> issue RS256 access token (15m) and refresh token (7d, rotates on use).

Apply: JWKS auth, RFC 7807 errors, ECS Fargate blue-green zero-downtime deploys, real PostgreSQL tests via testcontainers (no mocking), structured JSON logging, GitHub Actions + Claude Code review, Datadog monitoring, minimum 2 tasks across 3 AZs, Secrets Manager with 90-day rotation, and App Mesh/Envoy mTLS.
At 1500 req/min from one IP: API Gateway passes (below 5000 burst/2000 steady), ALB enforces 1000/min/IP, so ~500 req/min are rejected before hitting Auth containers.

JWT signing key dependencies: Auth Service private signing key, JWKS public endpoint, API Gateway Lambda authorizer verification, Portfolio verification, Notification verification, and JWKS LRU cache (1000 entries/5-minute TTL).

Commit -> Claude Code review -> merge -> GitHub Actions (lint, type-check, test with testcontainers, build, push to ECR) -> ECS Fargate blue-green deploy with ALB target-group switch -> run at min 2 tasks across 3 AZs -> Datadog APM in production.
